All right, the wolf is in the house here. Daily motivation slash skills training. Here's the deal. This is not an ordinary training today. You know why? That's special. The tonality of scarcity. I just low my voice for the tonality of scarcity. Immediately as soon as I do that, what did you do? You key into what I say. You can't help yourself. Can you lower your voice? It's not a whisper. It's not that. It's a power whisper. The oomph. I want you to have the oomph in your tonality that says, yeah, it's in short supply, but it's urgent. It's interesting because urgency is not its own tonality. It collapses into scarcity. Urgency is its close first cousin, like Danny and Nancy, from the movie Danny was first cousins with Nancy, my father. Scary. The point is their first cousin is those two of us. Scarcity urgency. Urgency is this idea that you have to do it now. And scarcity is, well, people want to take action now to get what is in short supply. So they go very much hand in hand. But in order to create urgency, it's the power side of scarcity. John, give me one shot. Right now. Right now, John. Right now. There's only five of these left. Listen, it's only five of these left versus there's only five of these left. You hear the difference? One of them is a power projecting outside of my body versus one's just a lower tone. Now, yes, the lower tone to itself will apply some scarcity, but that next level up, which also includes that informational scarcity. It's almost like the, like almost you're alluding to the fact that the information itself is in short supply. Don't ever forget the power of informational scarcity. Seriously. It's, you know, the example I always give is like, you know, on Wall Street, we all knew that insider trading was illegal. So while you do it, you almost always get caught. It's very easy to get caught. Right? Why do people do it? Because the allure of information that's secret, scarcity of information is very powerful and persuasive. People want to act on that. Same thing goes in sales. You can use it ethically in sales by using those tonality, the power whisper, apply urgency to the scarcity. Remember the three forms of scarcity, right? You have verbal scarcity, which is this is the only desk left. After this, they're all gone. Second will be total. It's only this desk and then they're all gone. Or there's only this one desk left and no one even knows there's only one left. I'm putting an ad in the paper tomorrow, but no one knows that yet. So you're applying, you're, you're, you're employing there is that because no one knows yet and there's an ad coming out, what's going to happen to Mamalong Creek, you'll be gone. Informational scarcity is the ticket. So if you can apply it to what you're selling, if you can at least use scarcity in the form of tonal scarcity. All right. Make sure you use this every single day this week. Don't settle for any less than the best tone out of you can muster and scarcity is a lynchpin tone out here. I love you guys. Go out there, kick some butt. The world is yours. Seriously. You make your own world. Go out there and take advantage of the gift you're getting from this training.
